title: lycopus lucidus var. americanus
intro: Lycopus lucidus var. americanus, also known as American Bugleweed or American Water Horehound, is a perennial herb native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, and to treat conditions such as fever, coughs, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Lycopus lucidus var. americanus include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve sleep quality, and reduce anxiety. Lycopus lucidus var. americanus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusing dried leaves in hot water, making it a versatile herb for various herbal preparations.
